posts.title: Intense debate about political science class
posts.body: We had a heated discussion in class today about controversial topics. Some students got really passionate and voices were raised. Is this normal for poli sci classes?
posts.author: Lion #EGXU

posts.title: This professor is terrible and should be fired
posts.body: Prof X in the CS department is the worst. They don't answer emails, grade unfairly, and clearly don't care about students. Avoid at all costs!!!
posts.author: Lion #MPN8

posts.title: Feeling overwhelmed and hopeless lately...
posts.body: Everything feels like too much. I don't know what to do anymore. Nothing seems to matter and I can't see things getting better. Has anyone else felt this way?
posts.author: Lion #PMYR

posts.title: Looking for exam answers - will pay!!
posts.body: [Content removed by CU moderators for policy violations]
posts.author: Lion #FHD0
posts.redacted_body: Anyone have answers for tomorrow's final? Can Venmo you $50. DM me ASAP.

posts.title: Part-time job recommendations on campus?
posts.body: Looking for a part-time job that won't kill my GPA. What campus jobs have good pay and reasonable hours? Bonus if I can do homework during slow periods lol
posts.author: Lion #5NFW

answers.body: library jobs are the move. lots of downtime for studying
answers.author: Lion #4QGT

answers.body: tutoring through the writing center pays well if ur good at writing
answers.author: Lion #VLBE

answers.body: residential life desk attendant is pretty chill. night shifts are quiet
answers.author: Lion #35DZ

posts.title: Good spots for quiet studying near campus?
posts.body: Butler is always packed and I can't focus in my dorm. Where do people go to study when the libraries are full? Bonus points if they have good coffee nearby.
posts.author: Lion #FVS7

answers.body: Try the law library - it's less crowded than Butler and super quiet. game changer fr
answers.author: Lion #D67R

comments.body: agreed leetcode is necessary evil. hackerrank is also good for practice
comments.author: Lion #FVS7

answers.body: Joe Coffee in the journalism building has great atmosphere and usually has seats.
answers.author: Lion #AGTI

answers.body: Milstein at Barnard is nice too! Open to Columbia students.
answers.author: Lion #SX29

answers.body: unpopular opinion but the business school library is underrated. less crowded after 5pm
answers.author: Lion #UCXG

comments.body: xi'an is SO good. the spicy cumin lamb is my go-to
comments.author: Lion #AGTI

answers.body: Hungarian Pastry Shop on Amsterdam is my go-to. Good coffee, no one bothers you.
answers.author: Lion #CIJH

posts.title: Best CS electives for junior year?
posts.body: I'm a CS major trying to plan my schedule for next semester. Already took OS and algorithms. What electives would you recommend? Thinking about ML, NLP, or computer graphics. Which ones have reasonable workloads?
posts.author: Lion #WPUM

answers.body: Graphics is super fun but time-consuming. Be ready to spend a lot of time on the ray tracer project. totally worth it tho if ur into that stuff
answers.author: Lion #BR9Y

comments.body: wait how time consuming are we talking? like more than OS?
comments.author: Lion #MRGG

answers.body: NLP with Prof McKeown was one of my favorite classes. Final project lets you work on something you're actually interested in.
answers.author: Lion #K7ZL

answers.body: tbh security is lowkey slept on. really practical stuff and not as much math as ML
answers.author: Lion #MRGG

answers.body: Highly recommend ML! Prof Collins is great and the projects are interesting. Workload is manageable if you stay on top of readings.
answers.author: Lion #F4QT

comments.body: seconding this! ML projects are actually fun
comments.author: Lion #RH5V

posts.title: How to deal with a difficult roommate situation?
posts.body: My roommate has been having people over super late every night and it's affecting my sleep and grades. We've talked about it but nothing changes. What are my options? Is it too late to switch rooms?
posts.author: Lion #QKHE

answers.body: document everything and talk to your RA. they can help mediate the situation
answers.author: Lion #CXJG

answers.body: had the same issue freshman year. ended up switching rooms through housing and it was the best decision ever
answers.author: Lion #UKBX

answers.body: try the roommate agreement form - it sounds silly but having things in writing actually helps
answers.author: Lion #VW1R

posts.title: How competitive is the pre-med track here?
posts.body: Thinking about going pre-med but I've heard horror stories about the competitiveness and curve. Is it really that bad? Or are people exaggerating? I don't want to burn out before I even apply to med school
posts.author: Lion #VC7M

answers.body: the curve is real in some classes but not all. orgo is rough, bio is more fair
answers.author: Lion #ORIU

answers.body: its competitive but not cutthroat if u find the right people. study groups help a lot
answers.author: Lion #PHWH

answers.body: honestly the hardest part is juggling clinical experience + research + classes + social life. time management is key
answers.author: Lion #XP5V

posts.title: gym way too crowded - alternatives?
posts.body: dodge is literally packed 24/7 and i hate waiting for equipment. does anyone know any affordable gyms near campus? or maybe less crowded times to go? trying to stay consistent with working out but this is making it so hard
posts.author: Lion #GEVZ

answers.body: planet fitness on 125th is like $10/month and 24 hours. worth it for the convenience
answers.author: Lion #UC6Y

comments.body: the orgo curve saved my life fr
comments.author: Lion #AEHZ

answers.body: also check out the smaller barnard gym if u have access. less crowded
answers.author: Lion #C3LH

answers.body: try going before 7am or after 9pm. way less crowded at those times
answers.author: Lion #J02F

posts.title: Wien vs Schapiro for sophomores?
posts.body: Got my housing assignment and have to choose between Wien and Schapiro. Any thoughts on which is better? Heard Wien has bigger rooms but Schapiro is newer. Also wondering about the social scene in each.
posts.author: Lion #W5R3

answers.body: pro tip: if u can get a corner room in wien its actually pretty nice. more natural light
answers.author: Lion #LJBF

answers.body: Schapiro has way better lounges and study spaces. Wien's common areas are kind of meh.
answers.author: Lion #NJCQ

comments.body: the law library tip is gold. tysm
comments.author: Lion #W5R3

answers.body: Wien for sure. Bigger rooms make a huge difference. The laundry situation is similar in both.
answers.author: Lion #PSG9

comments.body: omg yes hungarian pastry shop is so underrated
comments.author: Lion #ENMU

answers.body: Lived in both - Wien freshman year, Schapiro sophomore year. Honestly both are fine, location is the same.
answers.author: Lion #VJ7A

posts.title: Professor recs for Intro Psych?
posts.body: Need to fulfill a science requirement and thinking about Intro Psych. Who's good? I've heard mixed things about different professors. Looking for someone engaging who doesn't have impossible exams.
posts.author: Lion #H4UZ

answers.body: Avoid the Thursday section - it conflicts with a lot of other popular classes.
answers.author: Lion #5UMR

answers.body: the TAs for this class are super helpful during office hours btw
answers.author: Lion #P2GH

comments.body: SURF is amazing! highly recommend applying
comments.author: Lion #94MJ

answers.body: Take it with Prof Metcalfe if you can. She's tough but fair and her lectures are actually interesting.
answers.author: Lion #KZAT

posts.title: Safe late-night food delivery options?
posts.body: Sometimes I'm studying super late and get hungry around 2am. What delivery options are actually safe and reliable at that hour? Had some sketchy experiences with random apps
posts.author: Lion #PSRN

answers.body: JJ's is open late and takes meal swipes. the quesadillas are solid
answers.author: Lion #MMTZ

answers.body: uber eats usually has halal carts delivering late. might be sketchy quality tho lol
answers.author: Lion #RFZE

answers.body: insomnia cookies delivers until 3am and accepts dining dollars
answers.author: Lion #P1JV

posts.title: Research opportunities for freshmen?
posts.body: I'm a first-year interested in getting involved in research, specifically in neuroscience. Is it realistic to start this early? How do I even approach professors about this? Any tips would be appreciated!
posts.author: Lion #PZL5

answers.body: some profs prefer students who have taken relevant courses but others are happy to train you from scratch
answers.author: Lion #KNTI

answers.body: definitely possible as a freshman! email professors whose research interests you with a specific reason why
answers.author: Lion #3JRR

comments.body: insomnia cookies is dangerous for my wallet ngl
comments.author: Lion #2DBR

answers.body: SURF program in the summer is great for getting started. applications usually open in spring
answers.author: Lion #NLZ2

posts.title: Club recommendations for shy freshmen?
posts.body: Just started here and finding it hard to meet people. What clubs are welcoming to people who are a bit introverted? Looking for something low-key but social.
posts.author: Lion #ULER

answers.body: Photography club is great for introverts - you can just walk around and take photos together.
answers.author: Lion #71PR

answers.body: quiz bowl is surprisingly chill despite sounding intense lol. good people
answers.author: Lion #OV99

comments.body: did housing give u a hard time about switching rooms mid semester?
comments.author: Lion #NZLE

answers.body: Check out the board game club! Super chill vibes, no pressure to talk if you don't want to.
answers.author: Lion #QLUY

comments.body: facts irving farm is worth the walk
comments.author: Lion #BYKR

answers.body: Volunteering clubs like CU in the City are nice because you're focused on the activity, not small talk.
answers.author: Lion #PXY6

posts.title: Dealing with midterm stress
posts.body: Feeling really overwhelmed with 3 midterms next week. Does anyone have tips for managing stress during exam season? Also wondering if the counseling center is hard to get appointments at.
posts.author: Lion #M20O

answers.body: The app Calm has free access through Columbia. The sleep meditations are fire honestly
answers.author: Lion #KGBK

answers.body: Counseling center appointments can take a week or two but they have same-day crisis appointments. Definitely reach out!
answers.author: Lion #GEFI

answers.body: also dont forget to eat properly during exam season. it sounds basic but it makes a huge difference
answers.author: Lion #B02R

comments.body: board game club sounds perfect for me actually. when do they meet?
comments.author: Lion #KGBK

answers.body: Exercise helps me a lot. Even just a 20 min walk around campus clears my head.
answers.author: Lion #FWMH

posts.title: anyone else lowkey struggling in orgo rn??
posts.body: ngl i thought i was prepared but this class is hitting different. the exam average was a 52 and i somehow got below that lmao. anyone wanna study together or know good tutoring resources? im literally desperate at this point
posts.author: Lion #QUDM

answers.body: ngl i failed the first exam and still ended up with a B+. the curve is real dont give up
answers.author: Lion #MCCK

comments.body: wait art hum museum visits sound fun. which museums do you go to?
comments.author: Lion #UMN5

answers.body: honestly organic chemistry tutor on youtube saved my grade. watch his videos before lecture
answers.author: Lion #A9O5

answers.body: study groups are essential for this class. solo studying doesnt work for most ppl
answers.author: Lion #BCKX

answers.body: the orgo tutoring at milstein is free and actually helpful! go to the sunday sessions
answers.author: Lion #FRLY

posts.title: OPT application timeline question
posts.body: International student here. Planning to apply for OPT after graduation in May. When should I start the application process? Also, does ISSO have walk-in hours or do I need an appointment?
posts.author: Lion #03X3

answers.body: Definitely book an ISSO appointment ASAP. They'll walk you through everything. The online portal is confusing.
answers.author: Lion #3O8Q

comments.body: the linkedin alumni thing actually worked for me. got 3 interviews from cold messages
comments.author: Lion #DEM1

answers.body: Start at least 90 days before graduation! ISSO appointments fill up fast during spring semester.
answers.author: Lion #UOTU

answers.body: dont stress too much, the ISSO advisors are super helpful. just make sure u have all ur documents ready
answers.author: Lion #DEM1

posts.title: Selling textbooks - Calc III and Orgo
posts.body:
Selling my textbooks from last semester:
- Stewart Calculus (8th ed) - $40
- Klein Organic Chemistry (3rd ed) - $50

Both in good condition with minimal highlighting. Can meet on campus.
posts.author: Lion #UFO9

posts.title: Financial aid appeal process?
posts.body: My family's financial situation changed significantly this year. Has anyone gone through the financial aid appeal process? How long does it usually take and what documentation did you need?
posts.author: Lion #O1NS

posts.title: best spots to take a nap on campus?
posts.body: ok hear me out... sometimes u just need a power nap between classes. where are the best hidden spots to catch some Zs without being judged?? asking for myself not even gonna lie
posts.author: Lion #JOAY

answers.body: the lawn on a nice day. just bring a blanket and set an alarm lol
answers.author: Lion #ARXR

answers.body: butler 209 has those comfy chairs in the back. prime napping real estate
answers.author: Lion #B5YV

answers.body: controversial but the religion library is so quiet u will definitely fall asleep
answers.author: Lion #2RM7

answers.body: lerner piano lounge when its empty is elite. the couches are surprisingly comfortable
answers.author: Lion #ANXG

posts.title: Best lunch spots near Lerner?
posts.body: Getting tired of JJ's and Ferris. What are some good affordable lunch options walking distance from campus? Preferably under $15.
posts.author: Lion #OVL0

answers.body: Dig Inn on Broadway is healthy and portions are huge. Around $12-14.
answers.author: Lion #CRBV

answers.body: Shake Shack on 86th isn't walking distance but worth the subway ride honestly.
answers.author: Lion #MWKP

comments.body: organic chemistry tutor is goated. wish i found him earlier
comments.author: Lion #X5HV

answers.body: xi'an famous foods on broadway is amazing if u like spicy chinese food. the biang biang noodles are elite
answers.author: Lion #96A8

answers.body: Mel's Burger Bar has great burgers and student discounts on Tuesdays!
answers.author: Lion #VANL

posts.title: Summer internship search tips?
posts.body: Junior looking for SWE internships for this summer. Applied to like 50 companies and only heard back from 3. Any advice on improving my resume or finding companies that are still hiring?
posts.author: Lion #BFYW

answers.body: Make sure your resume is ATS-friendly. I got way more callbacks after reformatting mine.
answers.author: Lion #UD3J

answers.body: leetcode grind is unfortunately necessary but start with easys and work ur way up
answers.author: Lion #FYEC

answers.body: Some companies do late hiring rounds in Feb/March. Don't give up!
answers.author: Lion #0YFN

answers.body: ngl the startup career fair is slept on. less competitive and they actually respond to apps
answers.author: Lion #AQ0C

comments.body: this gives me hope lol. currently sitting at a 45 on the first exam
comments.author: Lion #UOUJ

answers.body: Have you tried reaching out to alumni on LinkedIn? A lot of people are willing to refer. CCE can help with this too.
answers.author: Lion #1MML

posts.title: ODS accommodation experiences?
posts.body: Thinking about registering with ODS for my ADHD. What's the process like? Do professors generally respect the accommodations? Worried about being treated differently.
posts.author: Lion #LVSV

answers.body: The process was really straightforward for me. Had my documentation ready and got accommodations within 2 weeks.
answers.author: Lion #PIO3

answers.body: Most professors have been super understanding in my experience. Just email them at the start of the semester.
answers.author: Lion #P1UD

answers.body: also u can get note-taking accommodations which is clutch for lectures
answers.author: Lion #NHHU

comments.body: 7am gym crew rise up
comments.author: Lion #A6TJ

answers.body: Extended time on exams has been a lifesaver. Definitely worth registering.
answers.author: Lion #JL9Q

posts.title: WiFi issues in Carman?
posts.body: Is anyone else having terrible WiFi in Carman lately? My connection keeps dropping during Zoom calls. Already tried resetting my router. Should I contact CUIT?
posts.author: Lion #LNWY

posts.title: Is double majoring worth it?
posts.body: I'm thinking about adding a second major (CS + Econ) but I'm worried about the workload. Did anyone here double major? Was it worth it or do you regret it? Trying to figure out if employers even care
posts.author: Lion #2BXJ

answers.body: did CS + Math, workload was manageable bc of overlap. econ has less overlap so might be harder
answers.author: Lion #9D5I

answers.body: i regret double majoring tbh. wish i had more time for extracurriculars and just enjoying college
answers.author: Lion #OACO

answers.body: employers honestly dont care that much unless its a very relevant combo. focus on internships instead
answers.author: Lion #I1CX

posts.title: Best coffee on campus? Need caffeine recommendations
posts.body: tbh im spending way too much money on coffee but i need it to survive. where do yall get your caffeine fix? looking for something thats not too expensive but also not garbage quality lol
posts.author: Lion #MUAD

answers.body: hot take but the dining hall coffee with some oat milk isnt that bad if ur broke
answers.author: Lion #AFZU

answers.body: Blue Java in Lerner is decent and accepts meal swipes. not the best but convenient
answers.author: Lion #JTKM

answers.body: Joe Coffee is pricey but worth it imo. their lattes are actually good
answers.author: Lion #I7JX

answers.body: irving farm on broadway is my go-to. a bit of a walk but the vibes are immaculate
answers.author: Lion #19EV

comments.body: library jobs are clutch. applied for one last semester
comments.author: Lion #DS3F

posts.title: What's the deal with Core Curriculum?
posts.body: Incoming freshman here trying to understand the Core requirements. Do most people actually enjoy these classes or is it just something to get through? Also which Core classes are the best/worst?
posts.author: Lion #8DD2

answers.body: lit hum is honestly great if you get a good section. frontiers of science is rough tho ngl
answers.author: Lion #UWPW

comments.body: butler 209 is my secret spot too lmao
comments.author: Lion #BQVA

answers.body: art hum is a nice break from other classes. museum visits are actually fun
answers.author: Lion #L1DX

answers.body: CC varies a lot depending on professor. ask around before picking sections
answers.author: Lion #02B0

comments.body: the religion library is TOO quiet. i get anxious there
comments.author: Lion #AILP

answers.body: unpopular opinion but i actually loved UWriting. depends on the instructor tho
answers.author: Lion #BQVA

posts.title: Looking for Data Structures study group
posts.body: Anyone want to form a study group for COMS 3134? Midterm is in 3 weeks and I could use some help with trees and graphs. Usually free on Tuesday/Thursday evenings.
posts.author: Lion #LABD

answers.body: yoo im in the same boat. thursdays work for me. butler 3rd floor?
answers.author: Lion #VCQZ

comments.body: this! exercise is literally free therapy
comments.author: Lion #VNXJ

answers.body: I'm down! Also struggling with AVL trees. DM me your number?
answers.author: Lion #9GUM

comments.body: wait we can use the business school library??
comments.author: Lion #6IRU

answers.body: Can I join? I'm pretty good with graphs but need help with heaps.
answers.author: Lion #PI14
